This Privacy Notice and DSAR Workflow applies to Apex Meridian HealthBank, Inc., a US health‑banking issuer subject to CCPA, HIPAA, GLBA, Reg FD, and applicable exchange rules.
We collect information reasonably linkable to a consumer, household, or patient (e.g., patient portal IDs, role emails, device IDs, masked account tokens like ACC-0000), and we do not collect SSNs in this workflow (example: 000-00-0000 is invalid).
For education and operations, we maintain training materials titled CryptoPay Sandbox, ESG/Climate Playbook, Cyber Incident Tabletop, Insider-List 101, and Information-Barrier Basics; these materials do not contain live customer records or deal content.
Subscription preferences for our ESG/climate newsletter and analyst education webinars are processed as marketing data under CCPA; you may opt out without affecting services.
HIPAA-regulated patient data is used only for treatment, payment, and healthcare operations; GLBA customer data is used for account servicing, fraud monitoring, and required reporting.
DSARs (access, deletion, correction, opt-out) may be submitted via privacy@apexmeridian.example or 1-800-000-0000, and we verify requests using account reference codes (e.g., REF-AXM-12345) rather than government IDs.
We retain training logs for Insider-List 101 and Information-Barrier Basics for 2 years, and purge CryptoPay Sandbox artifacts within 30 days; retention clocks are policy-based and not tied to securities events.
Vendor processing occurs in the United States; HIPAA Business Associate Agreements and GLBA service provider contracts are in place, and no cross-border transfers are performed for this workflow.
We document cyber incident tabletop drill outputs in a non-production system, and they are redacted to prevent tipping or selective disclosure risks in accordance with Reg FD guidance.
Public materials, such as our already-announced 20XX ESG report and a general cyber hygiene brochure, may reuse terms like “insider-list” and “blackout window” in a generic, educational sense.
Commercial-term signals is a product label for internal dashboards that summarize publicly available fee schedules and does not create or reflect binding pricing.
Prior to any public Form 8‑K or press release, access to preliminary breach assessment metrics is limited to employees on the Insider List under an information barrier, with audit logging and blackout enforcement.
We will not respond to DSARs seeking copies of invalid securities identifiers (e.g., “CUSIP: XXX-000-ZZZ”), marketing drafts, or hypothetical crypto wallet screenshots used in training.
This notice does not grant additional contractual rights and is designed to explain how we handle PII under the CCPA/HIPAA/GLBA patchwork for employees, patients, and analyst-facing materials.
Draft commercial term schedules for a not‑yet‑announced provider partnership are sequestered behind an information barrier and excluded from DSAR scope until publicly disclosed.
